observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Accounting Office, in coordination with the General Service, continuously reconcile those PPEs not supported with PPELCs in order to determine its accurate balances and corresponding depreciation, and same be prepared/maintained with respective PPELCs to ensure that balances of PPEs appearing on the financial statements are duly supported and substantiated;
The Appraisal and Inventory Committee conduct physical count of all PPEs including PPEs like Land, Land Improvements, Infrastructure Assets, and Buildings and Other Structures; and
Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts by following the guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. To ensure implementation thereto, create a team or committee who shall focus on the proper execution of the aforementioned circular with a concrete timeline.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the actual RPT and SET Receivables using the amounts per Realty Tax Delinquency Listing as of December 31, 2024 prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer;
The Municipal Treasurer provide duly Certified List of due and collectible taxes for the year to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of each year; and
The Municipal Accountant set-up at the beginning of the succeeding years the RPT and SET Receivables and corresponding Deferred RPT and SET Income based on the certified list from the Municipal Treasurer of the amount due and collectible for the year.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant expedite the tracing/finding of MOAs covering the unutilized fund transfers presented in Annex I, and/or coordinate with the SA to determine its proper disposition. Otherwise, revert the unutilized balances to the SA in accordance with existing rules and regulations;
Ensure remittance to the Bureau of Treasury of the cadastral fees and livestock/cattle fees accumulated in the Trust Fund; and
The Municipal Accountant make appropriate adjusting journal entry for the noted erroneous recording of incentives/rewards by debiting Due to NGAs account and crediting Prior Period Adjustment account amounting to ₱30,000.00.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Adopt austerity measures by reducing expenses or implement only those program/projects/activities that are critical, urgent and necessary and exercise prudence in the use of the limited cash resources;
Strengthen and improve financial performance through strategic measures to increase revenue collections from tax and non-tax revenues including its MEEDO operations; and
Create concrete plans to avoid incurrence of net loss in its future operations in order to improve the financial performance as well as condition of the municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs under continuing appropriations so that the same shall be considered in the utilization of the overall LDRRMF; and
Expedite the implementation of unutilized balances of the related PPAs under continuing appropriation, while the unutilized balances of those PPAs no longer feasible for implementation or PPAs already completed shall be made available for other DRRM activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer agreed to maintain the prescribed ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, for each type of inventory, to keep track of the actual Supplies Inventory on hand and reconcile the balances of the two inventory records

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Conduct due diligence in the evaluation of the contract time extensions prior approval thereof and ensure that reasons given to support the request are reasonable and justifiable that would not entail disadvantage to the government;
Ensure that BAC transact only with authorized supplier and dealer whose business permit or line of business is in line with the project involved and/or related to the item/s being procured to avoid risk on the quality, warranty, delivery, or service issues; and
The BAC and Municipal Accountant require the contractors/suppliers to indicate the date of their conformity with the terms and agreement of the contract/PO as this serve as basis in the computation of liquidated damages in case of delay; and

The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant reconcile the noted difference of ₱6.959 million between their records of Cash Local Treasury account and effect the necessary and applicable adjustments/
corrections;

The Municipal Treasurer update the cashbooks for Cash in Bank accounts and reconcile the same with the accounting’s records. A copy of the reconciliation made should be furnished to this Office for transparency and aide in audit; and

The Municipal Accountant maintain the SLs in harmony with the cashbooks of the Municipal Treasurer and prepare and maintain SLs for each accountable officer/collecting clerk of the municipality.

The Municipal Accountant trace up the cause of the negative balances of the account under the SEF and TF. Should it be established that there is over remittance, make representation with the Bureau of Revenue for the refund thereof, otherwise a Notice of Charge will be issued thereon.

The GSO include in the preparation of PC other PPEs like Land, Land Improvements, Infrastructure Assets and Buildings and Other Structures in the PC, and coordinate with the Accounting Office the cost of those constructed PPEs, if not available, request the Property Inventory, Appraisal, and Disposal Committee to determine its market value.

In the succeeding years, the personnel assigned present the Annual Investment Plan per thematic areas such as allocation for QRF, allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness and indicate therein the funding source whether it is from STF, continuing appropriation or the current LDRRMF.

The Municipal Accountant expedite the retrieval/tracing up and validation of these receivable accounts in order to establish its validity and effect its proper collection from the concerned accountable officer and/or individual;


Take the initiative to request for authority from COA to write-off and/or make the necessary adjustment of the account balances supported with the required documents as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

The Municipal Accountant review the validity of the unliquidated obligations or accounts payable which are outstanding for more than two years and if found to have no valid claims against the local government unit, cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus.

The Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer coordinate closely in monitoring the cash position of the municipality to ensure that all disbursements are properly manage and control to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft and obligations be paid accordingly.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant reconcile the noted difference of ₱12.888 million between cashbooks and ledgers, respectively, and effect the necessary and applicable adjustments/corrections, if any.
The Municipal Accountant include in the preparation of BRS the reconciliation for cashbook balances;
The Municipal Accountant furnish the bank a copy of the BRS and inform them of the items requiring adjustments, coupled with complete supporting documents, if any; and
Henceforth, ensure that regular and timely reconciliation between the records of the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant is strictly observed so that errors or any other reconciling items will be immediately corrected/updated.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Pursuant to the Guidelines on Termination of Contracts as provided in Annex “I” of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, officially terminate the contract with the defaulting contractors and imposed the proper sanctions or administrative penalties, as warranted, as well as to initiate the forfeiture of the performance security posted by the concerned contractors in accordance with Appendix 17 of the Revised IRR; and
Revert the remaining contract cost to the unobligated appropriation and re-evaluate the remaining unaccomplished scope of works if may still be feasible to be continued through take-over contracts, if not, revert the same to the unappropriated surplus.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Planning and Development Council prepare specific program, projects and activities in the 20% DF Plan for itemized appropriations in the Annual Budget, otherwise, secure first approval or authorization from the SB prior to implementation of PPAs funded out of the lump sum appropriations.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Solid Waste Management Board (SWMB) and its Technical Working Group (TWG) discuss the best action plan to be taken on the deficiencies noted in order to remedy the same and/prevent further loss or wastage of government funds/resources, notwithstanding, any sanction that may be impose to those responsible for a wasteful project implementation if warranted;
The SWMB and TWG give due consideration on the sequential function of each programs, projects, activities (PPAs) in solid waste management for proper prioritization of its implementation and to avoid non-functional projects and wastage of government facility/resources; and
Revisit the identified roles of the TWG in the 10-Year SWMP and require its members to properly observe and play their respective roles in solid waste management so as to effectively and efficiently plan, prioritize and systematically implement its identified PPAs.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Stop the charging from LDRRMF of expenses that are administrative in nature and are not directly related to disaster risk and management activities to avoid issuance Notice of Suspensions or Disallowances; and
Comply with the monthly submission of Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Expedite the tracing/finding of MOAs covering the long outstanding unutilized balances of fund transfers presented in Annex L and/or coordinate with the SA to determine its proper disposition. Otherwise, revert the unutilized balances to the SA in accordance with existing rules and regulations;
Expedite the updating of the MOA with the DOH pertaining to the ₱250,000.00 UHC grant for its timely implementation and enjoyment of its intended benefits to the constituents; and
Utilize the biometric system/machine in rendering overtime services in order to reflect the transparent and correct man-hours/attendance actually rendered by LGU personnel and achieve the intended purpose of the system.

We recommended and the Management agreed to refrain from charging inappropriate expenditures in the PCSO Lotto Fund and ensure that only medical and health-care related programs and charity services are charged therefrom that will directly benefit the intended beneficiaries, in line with the PCSO charter and requirement of RA No. 1169.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant submit the DVs of cash advances for CF to the concerned COA ATL within seven days after release of check for post-audit in a sealed envelope with covering transmittal letter enumerating the supporting documents; and
Ensure compliance with Section 6.1.8.4 of the aforementioned joint circular and that all the required documents to support the DV for the grant of CF are completely attached/submitted.

We recommended and the management agreed to ensure that the mandated function of the MPDC and Municipal Engineer as mandated by the local government code are properly observed in the implementation of programs, projects and activities of the municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The BAC secure copies of Mayor’/Business Permit and PhilGEPS Registration Number in its procurement thru shopping prior recommendation to HOPE to award the contract; and
The Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements are properly supported with complete documentation by requiring the submission of Mayor’/Business Permit and PhilGEPS Registration Number of participating suppliers in case the municipality resorted to Shopping as its mode of procurement.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Immediately remit the remaining withheld taxes of prior months and
ensure henceforth, that all the taxes withheld are remitted intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges; and
Deduct from the withholding tax of the concerned supplier the over-remittance of ₱1,617.35 under Special Education Fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accounting and Treasurer’s Offices immediately remit the remaining withheld GSIS contributions and/or loan amortizations of prior months under GF-Proper and ensure henceforth, that all withholdings for same are remitted intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges;
The Municipal Accounting Office expedite the tracing up and reconciliation of the over remittances.  Should it be established that there is indeed over remittance, payment from the concerned/liable personnel should be enforced; and
Trace up the details of the Due to GSIS balance of ₱8,026.68 under Trust Fund and make the necessary adjustments, as appropriate.

Provide sufficient information in the succeeding Notes to Financial Statements for long-term Loans Payable to give additional value to the financial statements of the LGU that facilitate decision-making to its users or other stakeholders

Include in the budget preparation budgetary requirements for personal services, maintenance and other operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the Office of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management. Pending compliance of the foregoing recommendation, disbursements relative thereof shall be charged against the general fund of the LGU.

Expedite the request for the cancellation of the bank service charges totaling to ₱40,429.78. Otherwise, these will be the personal liability and should be refunded by9 the accountable officer (s) liable for its incurrence.

The MSWDO trace up/reconcile the unaccounted balance of 3,883 sacks of rice and require the concerned officials to have the signature of the 283 recipients to acknowledge their receipt of the relief goods and have the RDSs be duly signed by the concerned/in-charge officials.

The Municipal Accountant record the corresponding receivables from the Realty Tax Delinquencies and prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for RPT/SET Receivables based on the certified list of taxpayers and its corresponding amount due and collectibles from the Municipal Treasurer. If warranted, secure link with the ETRACS for more efficient maintenance of accounting records on RPT/SET Receivables.
Prioritize the implementation of the immediate rehabilitation and closure of the Municipal Disposal Site following the conditions embodied in the Authority to Close issued by the DENR; and

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to record the corresponding receivables from the Realty Tax Delinquencies and prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for RPT/SET Receivables based on the certified list of taxpayers and its corresponding amount due and collectibles from the Municipal Treasurer. If warranted, secure link with the eTRACS for more efficient maintenance of accounting records on RPT/SET Receivables.
observations.reason: Certified list of taxpayers and its corresponding amount due and collectibles were not yet forwarded by the Municipal Treasurer to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Accountant trace back all inventories already issued based on the report submitted by the General Services Office and make the appropriate adjusting entries to properly account the same in the books of the Municipality; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant record immediately all issuances of inventory upon receipt of SSMI together with the Requisition and Issue Slip to avoid reoccurrence of the same deficiency.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer transfer the unexpended balance of the previous years’ appropriation for CYs 2020 to 2021 in the STF less the unexpended balances that were previously transferred based on actual receipts, and the Municipal Accountant make the appropriate adjusting entry using TL-DRRMF account; and
Henceforth, transfer to STF should be based on the unexpended balance of the appropriated QRF and DRRMF-MOOE.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
Require the Local Covid Facility operated by MEEDO to refund the overpayment of meals amounting to ₱232,650.00; and
The Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements are properly supported with complete documentation and discontinue the practice of processing of payment for claims that are not sufficiently documented to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
The LDRRMO and Municipal Accountant submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization on DRRMF on or before the 15th day of each month as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012;
The LDRRMO and MPDC exercise prudence in preparing LDRRMFIP and AIP/Supplemental AIP, respectively, and to itemize the PPAs and its corresponding budget allocation in preparing the said reports and ensure its proper budget classification; and
The LDRRMO and MPDC be reminded that the LDRRMFIP and AIP are interrelated planning records, hence, they should be prepared in relation to the other and must supply coherent/consistent information for more effective and efficient planning and be prepared in such a way not to confuse readers or stakeholders.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
The Municipal Treasurer make adjustment in his cashbook amounting to ₱100,000.00 as the same has been outstanding for over six months from date of issue and considered staled check;
The Municipal Accountant make adjustment in her book for the documentary stamp tax of ₱33,999.78 charged from Special Savings Account; and
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records/books to determine any necessary corrections and henceforth, to conduct a regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected either by the Treasury or Accounting Office will be timely adjusted and posted in their respective books.

We recommended and the management agreed that transfer from the appropriate budget of the General Fund be made to restore the balance of the BGCM Fund and that the following adjusting entry be made by the Municipal Accountant:
Prior Year’s adjustment ₱ 84,377.90
Due to NGAs ₱ 84,377.90 (To recognize liability account for the unutilized balance of BGCM Fund which was initially recorded as Grants and Donations)
Prior Year’s adjustment ₱ 83,560.00
Due to Other Fund-BGCM Fund ₱ 83,560.00 (To record intra-agency payable account in the General Fund-Proper for the GF expenses inappropriately charged against the BGCM Fund)
Due from Other Fund-General Fund Proper ₱83,560.00
Prior Year’s adjustment ₱ 83,560.00 (To record intra-agency receivable account in the General Fund-BGCM Fund for the GF expenses inappropriately charged against the BGCM Fund)

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
The personnel assigned in the receipts and distribution of the donations in-kind trace out all documents pertaining thereto in order to establish the total quantity and amount of items distributed to beneficiaries and to determine the balances, if any, for proper recording/accounting thereof;
The personnel assigned revise the Summary/List of Donations Receives, Distributed and Balances to properly categorize the donated items as to food, medical supplies, medicines, etc. and provide quantities of items distributed and the corresponding balances per item; and
Comply with the reportorial requirements of COA Circular No. 2020-009 for proper transparency and accountability in the used/distribution of donated items to the Municipality.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
The LDRRM Council ensure that the LDRRMFIP, LDRRM Plan and AIP will be prepared in relation to the other, supply coherent/consistent information for more effective and efficient planning and be prepared in such a way not to confuse readers or stakeholders;
In the succeeding years, the personnel assigned present the Annual Investment Plan per thematic areas such as allocation for QRF, allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness and indicate therein the funding source whether it is from STF, continuing appropriation or the current LDRRMF; and
Ensure that the required reports are prepared by the proper officials concerned, in accordance with the prescribed format and guidelines, and submitted within the reglementary period; and
The Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for each type of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to expedite the retrieval/tracing up and validation of these receivable accounts in order to establish its validity and effect its proper collection from the concerned accountable officer and/or individual; and
Take the initiative to request for authority from COA to write-off and/or make the necessary adjustment of the account balances supported with the required documents as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.reason: According to the Municipal Accountant, they have already conducted meeting with the DSWD regarding collectibles from various persons under the SEA (Self-Employment Assistance) Kaunlaran Program and ongoing validation of other receivable accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to review the validity of the unliquidated obligations or accounts payable which are outstanding for more than two years and if found to have no valid claims against the local government unit, cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus.
We recommended that Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the taxes withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make verification, analysis, and validation of the prior years’ unremitted Due to GSIS and make representations with the GSIS, if needed, for any reconciling items.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Direct the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of the physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE to ascertain its existence and condition and submit to the Audit Team the properly accomplished RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year;
Ensure that the RPCPPE were prepared by type for easy reconciliation; and
Reconciliation of related subsidiary records should be regularly undertaken and regarded with utmost importance in order to established the completeness and accuracy of the PPE as presented in the financial statements.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Require the Municipal Accountant to compute the correct depreciation expenses of its assets and a journal entry must be drawn to update/correct the accumulated depreciation by a debit to depreciation expense (for the current year depreciation), debit to prior year’s adjustment (for the prior year’s depreciation) and a credit to accumulated depreciation (for the total unrecorded depreciation);
Exert effort through the General Services Office/Office of the Municipal Treasurer, to reconstruct and update its property records and reconcile with the accounting records to facilitate the setting up and periodic provision of depreciation for those depreciable assets not provided with depreciation; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that all depreciable properties owned by the LGU are provided with depreciation in accordance with the above-mentioned rules and regulations to ensure its fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to require the Municipal Accountant to record the corresponding receivables from the Realty Tax Delinquencies and prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for RPT/SET Receivables based on the certified list of taxpayers and its corresponding amount due and collectibles from the Municipal Treasurer. If warranted, secure link with the eTRACS for more efficient maintenance of accounting records on RPT/SET Receivables.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:

Require the Municipal Accountant to expedite the retrieval/tracing up and validation of these receivable accounts in order to establish its validity and effect its proper collection from the concerned accountable officer and/or individual; and
Take the initiative to request for authority from COA to write-off and/or make the necessary adjustment of the account balances supported with the required documents as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to review the validity of the unliquidated obligations or accounts payable which are outstanding for more than two years and if found to have no valid claims against the local government unit, cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Ensure that the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer coordinate closely in monitoring the cash position of the municipality to ensure that all disbursements are properly manage and control to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft and obligations be paid accordingly;
Set aside the IRA received for 20 percent Development Fund to be used solely for purpose/s it was appropriated while the 5 percent of the estimated revenues should also be intact for payment of calamity related expenditures; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to work back and reconcile the balances to ensure that there are available resources for expenditures and the currently maturing obligations. Closely monitor the cash balance against expenditures to be incurred to avoid cash overdraft.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Engineer to conduct thorough review on the computation of ABC to ensure compliance with the provisions of DPWH Department Order No. 197 dated October 7, 2016 in the preparation of the ABC and submit justification/explanation on the deficiencies noted.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Ensure that the required reports are prepared by the proper officials concerned, in accordance with the prescribed format, and submitted within the reglementary period; and
Remind the concerned officials to be mindful of their corresponding roles in the administration of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund in order to enhance the agency’s capacity to strengthen its disaster risk reduction and management system, through decentralized powers, responsibilities and resources.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Direct the LDRRMO to prepare annually the LDRRMFIP for the DRRM projects, activities or program using the prescribed format as attached herein, taking into consideration the APB for LDRRM and the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements from LDRRMF are duly supported with LDRRMFIP; and
The LDRRM Council to be reminded that the LDRRMFIP, APB and AIP are interrelated planning records, hence, they should be prepared in relation to the other and must supply coherent/consistent information for more effective and efficient planning and be prepared in such a way not to confuse the readers or stakeholders.

1. For the inventory committee to regularly conduct the annual physical count of property, plant and equipment and prepare the corresponding report thereon; and
Thereafter, reconciliation of the RPCPPE be made by the Municipal Supply/Property Unit and the Accounting Office. Considering the huge amount and quantity to be reconciled, develop strategies, policies and procedures so that reconciliation of PPE can be made easier.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the agency’s receivable accounts and determine the debtors and/or implementing government entities concerned in case of fund transfers and to maintain and update individual subsidiary ledgers composing the accounts; and
If the subject receivable accounts were non-moving for 10 years or more, and settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained, request for its write-off following the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant receivable accounts under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2005.
We recommended that Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the taxes withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

We recommended that Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the contributions and loan amortizations withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant make verification, analysis, and validation of the prior years’ unremitted Due to GSIS and make representations with the GSIS, if needed, for any reconciling items.

We recommended for the management to direct the members of the LCPC to prepare the Local Development Plan for Children, Local Investment Plan for Children and Children’s Profile in compliance with the Section II.1 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

Payables aging 2 years and more without valid creditors/complete supporting documents be reverted to prior year’s adjustments; and
Refrain from issuing Purchase Orders at the last week of December of each year.
All cash advances be fully liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended the following:
1.	For the inventory committee to regularly conduct the annual physical count of property, plant and equipment and prepare the corresponding report thereon; and
2.	Thereafter, reconciliation of the RPCPPE be made by the Municipal Supply/Property Unit and the Accounting Office.  Considering the huge amount and quantity to be reconciled, develop strategies, policies and procedures so that reconciliation of PPE can be made easier.

We recommended the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the agency’s receivable accounts and determine the debtors and/or implementing government entities concerned in case of fund transfers and to maintain and update individual subsidiary ledgers composing the accounts; and
b. If the subject receivable accounts were non-moving for 10 years or more, and settlement/collectability could no longer be ascertained, request for its write-off following the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant receivable accounts under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2005.
We recommended that Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the taxes withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

We recommended that Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the contributions and loan amortizations withheld intact and within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges;
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant make verification, analysis, and validation of the prior years’ unremitted Due to GSIS and make representations with the GSIS, if needed, for any reconciling items.

We recommendedfor the management to direct the members of the LCPC to prepare the Local Development Plan for Children, Local Investment Plan for Children and Children’s Profile in compliance with the Section II.1 of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal GSO and Office of the Municipal Accountant to give preferential and full attention on the reconciliation of the discrepancy of ₱459.105 million. Likewise, we recommended that the management prepare and submit the revised Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment that has been reconciled between the Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards.

To avoid accumulation of reconciling items and to correct the financial records of the Municipality of Kalibo, we recommended to management the following measures: Direct the Accountant to prepare and effect the necessary adjustments on the unrecorded transactions/reconciling items identified in the Bank Reconciliation Statements.  In cases, wherein adjustments require supporting documents or action by person/s other than himself, communicate officially to the concerned official or employee to ensure their immediate action; Require the Accountant to inform and furnish the bank a copy of the Bank Reconciliation Statements on items that require adjustments by the bank; and Request copies of debit/credit memos from the bank to support the items requiring adjustments.

We recommended the following measures in order to ensure the validity of claims and immediate payment of the accounts payable: Set a cut-off date for the submission of purchase requests from the different offices of the Municipality every December of each year to give ample time for the procurement and ensure actual delivery before the end of December; Payables aging 2 years and more without valid creditors/complete supporting documents be reverted to prior year’s adjustments; Refrain from issuing Purchase Orders at the last week of December of each year; and All cash advances be fully liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through his Department Heads: Hasten the implementation of the programs and projects funded from the Trust funds to ensure optimization of the benefits by the intended beneficiaries; and Require or assign Official to closely monitor the fund releases coming from the National Government to ensure the speedy implementation of the projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal GSO and Office of the Municipal Accountant to give preferential and full attention on the reconciliation of the discrepancy of ₱459.105 million. Likewise, we recommended that the management prepare and submit the revised Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment that has been reconciled between the Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards.

To avoid accumulation of reconciling items and to correct the financial records of the Municipality of Kalibo, we recommendedto management the following measures:

Direct the Accountant to prepare and effect the necessary adjustments on the unrecorded transactions/reconciling items identified in the Bank Reconciliation Statements.  In cases, wherein adjustments require supporting documents or action by person/s other than himself, communicate officially to the concerned official or employee to ensure their immediate action;

Require the Accountant to inform and furnish the bank a copy of the Bank Reconciliation Statements on items that require adjustments by the bank; and

Request copies of debit/credit memos from the bank to support the items requiring adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through his Department Heads:

Hasten the implementation of the programs and projects funded from the Trust funds to ensure optimization of the benefits by the intended beneficiaries; and

Require or assign Official to closely monitor the fund releases coming from the National Government to ensure the speedy implementation of the projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer furnish us certified copies of the proposed project statement/program of works submitted to the Regional DOH as basis of the grant of funds that conformed to Section I. A. a. III of the MOA.








We required the accounting department to reclassify those items that were improperly taken up as Construction in Progress-Buildings and Structures to the accounts which they should properly belong.





We recommended that the properties and other supplies and materials be issued a corresponding ARE or ICS to ensure proper accountability of government properties and small tangible assets.

We recommended that management require the Engineer in charge of the demolition of the old Municipal building and the newly completed improvement of TB DOTs facility to account for the demolished materials that can still be put to good use such as the doors, galvanized iron, wooden beams and ceiling materials and the like.It was also recommended that proper planning be done by the Municipal officials to ensure efficient and effective utilization of funds for government projects.

We recommended that management instruct the requisitioning offices for supplies and materials, or equipment for disaster purposes to use the fund for the purpose it was intended and not for the regular programs/activities of the department or office concerned.

Likewise, require the Municipal Health Office to submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RMIS) with corresponding Request and Issue Slips (RIS) on the issued Hospital-Based Care Consumables and Medicines for disaster response and for 24-hour emergency operations totaling P193,000.00 for proper accounting and audit purposes.  A letter request was sent to the Office of the MHO.

We recommended the following measures:

Direct the Accountant to prepare and effect the necessary adjustments on the unrecorded transactions/reconciling items identified in the Bank Reconciliation Statements.  In cases, wherein adjustments require supporting documents or action by person/s other than himself,  he should communicate  officially to the concerned official or employee to ensure their immediate action;

Require the  Accountant to inform and furnish the bank a copy of the  Bank Reconciliation Statements on items that require adjustments by the bank; and

Request copies of debit/credit memos from the bank to support the items requiring adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and effect the necessary adjustments in their respective books of accounts in accordance with Section 59 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.   Likewise, in order to avoid or lessen the incurrence of stale checks, require the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check at least one month after its issuance or even shorter, if possible.

We recommended that the General Services Office, notify the Auditor of the time of the scheduled delivery of every procured goods regardless of amount and/or furnish the office of the Audit Team Leader photo copy of the delivery receipt within twenty-four hours from acceptance to allow the Auditor to exercise her prerogative function of inspection of the delivered goods.

We recommended that the Municipal General Services Office find time and exert extra effort in registering the vehicle with LTO and apply for the comprehensive vehicle insurance on the said vehicle in accordance with the stipulations of the Deed of Donation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal GSO and Office of the Municipal Accountant to give preferential and full attention on the reconciliation of the discrepancy of P270,906,911.01. Likewise, we recommend that management should prepare and submit the revised Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment, that has been reconciled between the stock cards and the ledger cards.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor through his department heads hasten the implementation of programs and projects funded from the Trust funds and the Development Fund of the Municipality to ensure optimization of the benefits by the intended beneficiaries of said programs and projects.  Likewise, require the Department Head, MPDCO to conduct regular monitoring of accomplishments versus the targeted programs and projects of the Municipality and submit the quarterly reports to the office of the Audit Team Leader, Team 4.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer furnish us certified copies of the proposed project statement/program of works submitted to the Regional DOH as basis of the grant of funds that conformed to Section I. A. a. III of the MOA.

We required the accounting department to reclassify those items that were improperly taken up as Construction in Progress-Buildings and Structures to the accounts which they should properly belong.

We recommended that the properties and other supplies and materials be issued a corresponding ARE or ICS to ensure proper accountability of government properties and small tangible assets.

We recommended that management require the Engineer in charge of the demolition of the old Municipal building and the newly completed improvement of TB DOTS facility to account for the demolished materials that can still be put to good use such as the doors, galvanized iron, wooden beams and ceiling materials and the like.

We also recommended that proper planning be done by the Municipal officials to ensure efficient and effective utilization of funds for government projects.

We recommended that management instruct the requisitioning offices for supplies and materials, or equipment for disaster purposes to use the fund for the purpose it was intended and not for the regular programs/activities of the department or office concerned.

Likewise, require the Municipal Health Office to submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RMIS) with corresponding Request and Issue Slips (RIS) on the issued Hospital-Based Care Consumables and Medicines for disaster response and for 24 hour emergency operations totaling P193,000.00 for proper accounting and audit purposes.  A letter request was sent to the Office of the MHO.

To avoid accumulation of reconciling items and to correct the financial records of the Municipality of Kalibo, we recommend the following measures:

Direct the Accountant to prepare and effect the necessary adjustments on the unrecorded transactions/reconciling items identified in the Bank Reconciliation Statements.  In cases, wherein adjustments require supporting documents or action by person/s other than himself,  he should communicate  officially to the concerned official or employee to ensure their immediate action;
Require the  Accountant to inform and furnish the bank a copy of the  Bank Reconciliation Statements on items that require adjustments by the bank; and
Request copies of debit/credit memos from the bank to support the items requiring adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and effect the necessary adjustments in their respective books of accounts in accordance with Section 59 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.   Likewise, in order to avoid or lessen the incurrence of stale checks, require the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payee of the existence of the check at least one month after its issuance or even shorter, if possible.

We recommended that the General Services Office, notify the Auditor of the time of the scheduled delivery of every procured goods regardless of amount and/or furnish the office of the Audit Team Leader photo copy of the delivery receipt within twenty-four hours from acceptance to allow the Auditor to exercise her prerogative function of inspection of the delivered goods.

We recommended that the Municipal General Services Office find time and exert extra effort in registering the vehicle with LTO and apply for the comprehensive vehicle insurance on the said vehicle in accordance with the stipulations of the Deed of Donation.

We recommend that the MGSO make constant follow-up with the LTO and/or inquire why it takes long in the approval of such application as basis in making a complaint for red tape or undue delay.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal GSO and Office of the Municipal Accountant to give preferential and full attention on the reconciliation of the discrepancy of P270,906,911.01. Likewise, we recommend that management should prepare and submit the revised Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment, that has been reconciled between the stock cards and the ledger cards.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor through his department heads hasten the implementation of programs and projects funded from the Trust funds and the Development Fund of the Municipality to ensure optimization of the benefits by the intended beneficiaries of said programs and projects.  Likewise, require the Department Head, MPDCO to conduct regular monitoring of accomplishments versus the targeted programs and projects of the Municipality and submit the quarterly reports to the office of the Audit Team Leader, Team 4.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor through his Legal Officer secure a legal decision as basis for incurring expenditures based on the 2015 Annual Budget declared “Inoperative in its Entirety” by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.
observations.managementAction: The Court issued Notice of Order dated April 19, 2016 in favor of the Municipality nullifying the SP’s resolution declaring the 2015 AB inoperative in its entirety.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the unused balances of completed projects be returned to the Source Agencies in accordance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.   While savings from donations for the victims of calamities maybe lumped together to come up with a bigger amount that can be used for similar/related purposes with the authority of legislative body or head of the agency having control thereof.
observations.managementAction: Some of the Savings were used for similar purposes, however, there are still balances that remained unimplemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal General Services Officer furnish the Office of the Municipal Accountant copies of donation papers and pertinent documents of the said ambulance as basis in recording the said property in the books of the municipality and for computing its depreciation cost.  A corresponding property ledger card and property card shall be maintained by both offices.

We recommend that the Municipal General Services Office register the vehicle with LTO and apply for the comprehensive vehicle insurance on the said vehicle in accordance with the stipulations of the Deed of Donation.

We recommend that the local Chief Executive requires the Municipal GSO and Office of the Municipal Accountant to give preferential and full attention on the reconciliation of the discrepancy of P 168,438,152.40.  Likewise, we recommend that management should prepare and submit the revised Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment, that has been reconciled between the stock cards and the ledger cards.

We recommend that the outstanding balances be returned back to the DSWD, Regional Office, or the Municipal officials make representation with the DSWD to disburse the funds to other victims of typhoon “Yolanda” that were not listed in the original list submitted in November 2014.
Hasten implementation of programs and projects under the Trust Fund to ensure optimization of the benefits by the beneficiaries of said programs and projects.
observations.managementAction:
There is a need to employ additional personnel due to the dynamic growth in the economy of the Municipality.

Reiterated as Finding No. 11
